# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.
# Category: container_security

```Dockerfile
# Start with an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3-slim  # replace this line if you want use other base images, e.g., node or alpine based on your requirement (e.g.: FROM python:alpine)

WORKDIR /app   # set the working directory in container to be "/app" inside docker image and it will serve as a default location for all subsequent commands 
# Copy local code to Docker image, so we can use 'docker run' again with our app. This is equivalent of "RUN git clone ..." command on your host machine   # replace this line if you want copy other files or directories from the source directory (e.g.: COPY . /app)
COPY ./src/uploads/. /app 
# Install production dependencies with npm and save them in requirements file for Docker image, equivalent of "RUN pip install -r requirement_file" on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other packages or libraries (e.g.: RUN apt-get update && apk add --no-cache nodejs)
COPY ./src/requirements/. /app 
# Install production dependencies in the Docker image, equivalent of "RUN pip install -r requirement_file" on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other packages or libraries (e.g.: RUN apt-get update && apk add --no-cache nodejs)
WORKDIR /app/src 
# Expose port for the app, equivalent of "EXPOSE" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other ports (e.g.: EXPOSE 80:80 or CMD [“http://+A:%PORT%”])
# Run application when container is started, equivalent of "ENTRYPOINT" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other commands (e.g.: ENV PATH /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin)
# Run the command as an entry point, equivalent of "CMD" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other commands (e.g.: CMD [“http://+A:%PORT%”])  or run python app inside container using 'docker exec'
# Run the command as an entry point, equivalent of "CMD" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other commands (e.g.: CMD [“http://+A:%PORT%”])  or run python app inside container using 'docker exec'
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "/app/src"]   
# Run the command as an entry point, equivalent of "CMD" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other commands (e.g.: CMD [“http://+A:%PORT%”])  or run python app inside container using 'docker exec'
# Run the command as an entry point, equivalent of "CMD" in Dockerfile on your host machine   # replace this line if you want to use other commands (e.g.: CMD [“http://+A:%PORT%”])  or run python app inside container using 'docker exec'
